name: Search for approved establishment (results + select)
pageTitle: Establishment - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Documents
heading: Search for an approved establishment
secondaryActions: Search for an approved establishment (add another)
layout: govuk-grid-row > govuk-grid-column-full (filter fields laid out in govuk-grid-column-one-third columns)

structure.text: Approved establishments search results table (visually-hidden table caption)
Columns: Name | Section | Type | Approval Number | Status | Country | Select
Select (one submit button per result row, name=add-id, value=establishment GUID)
Search for an approved establishment (re-opens the search to add another establishment)
Save and continue (terminal action — finalises the selected establishment(s))

govukComponents.usage: Page H1 'Search for an approved establishment'
Field labels for Country, Name, Approval number, Section, Type, Status, Sort by
Country, Section, Type, Status filters and Sort-by (order-by) select
Name and Approval number free-text filters
Desktop results table of approved establishments
Visible rule separating filter panel from results

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Each result row's 'Select' control is a <button type=submit name=add-id value={GUID} class=link-button> styled to look like a link, inside a per-row form; clicking submits the chosen establishment GUID and navigates to the establishment-of-origin page.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke 'button styled as link' class, not a Design System component. Functionally it is a per-row submit that carries the selected establishment id. The rebuild should model this as a normal choice action (radio + continue, or a govuk-button per row) rather than a custom link-button.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-button (or a Radios list of results with a single Continue button)
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 83 (buttons.txt; action 83 log shows button name=add-id id=select-establishment-1 value=11b4e363-...)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Wrapper class around the filter criteria panel.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom container styling, not a Design System component. Purely presentational; a govuk-grid-row/column layout can achieve the same.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: IPAFFS renders the results twice — a desktop govuk-table and a separate mobile card layout (paragraph-per-field) — toggled by CSS breakpoints. This is why 20 'Select' buttons exist for 10 visible rows (two forms of select-establishment-1..10).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke dual-render responsive table pattern with duplicated markup and duplicated submit buttons. Adds DOM weight and a11y duplication. The Design System responsive-table pattern (single table with data-labels) covers this without duplicate rendering.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-table with the GOV.UK responsive-table (stacking) pattern
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 83 (verified: 20 buttons with accessible name 'Select' in the before snapshot; controls.txt shows two identical add-id button sets select-establishment-1..10 — desktop form + mobile form)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: pagination / pagination-list / pagination-item / pagination-item-next / pagination-link / pagination-link-icon / pagination-link-label / pagination-link-title
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom pagination widget rendering 'Next page : 2 of 5' with an icon, linking to ?establishment-country-code=AF&page=2.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke pagination markup, not the Design System Pagination component (govuk-pagination). Predates or ignores the standard component.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 83 (nonstd.txt; a11y snapshot navigation 'Pagination' e196)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Prevents text wrapping (likely on the Select column / approval number cells).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Utility class outside the govuk-* namespace; low-risk but should use govuk-!-* utilities or table styling.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk table cell styling / govuk-!-* width utilities

fields.name: establishment-country-code
fields.label: Country (required)
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 83 (a11y snapshot combobox e64; controls.txt name=establishment-country-code). The selected first option is annotated '(country of origin)' — the list is defaulted to the notification's country of origin. Full list is country reference data — new app should source from a reference-data service, not hardcode. 'Not Found' is the final option. Page object confirms this is the required filter driving the search: SearchForApprovedEstablishmentPage.ts:10-12 (dropdownCountry = getByLabel('Country (required)')). Tests re-select the Country then Search to re-run: ched-p-workflows.ts:377-378, ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:114-116 (France value from country type).

fields.label: Name

fields.name: establishment-section
fields.label: Section
options: Blood and blood products or excluding of equidae or for technical purposes other than feed for animals
Blood and blood products, excluding of equidae, for technical purposes other than feed for animals
Establishments or plants carrying out intermediate activities and plants storing animal by-products
Establishments or plants for the storage of derived products
Establishments or plants handling animal by-products or derived products for purposes outside the feed chain
Establishments or plants manufacturing organic fertilisers or soil improvers
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 83 (a11y combobox e77; controls.txt name=establishment-section). First 20 options in DOM order (verified against the rendered <select>). Establishment reference data — 57 sections; the IUU-relevant 'Fishery products' (index 16) / 'Fishery products - EU' (index 17) appear inside the first 20, but other fishery-adjacent sections e.g. 'Live bivalve molluscs' (index 26), 'Meat products' (index 30), 'Slaughterhouses' (index 50) fall beyond it. New app should source from reference-data service. 'Not Found' is present as an option (mid-list, not final).

fields.label: Type
options: Alternative method: Multiple-step catalytic hydro-treatment for the production of renewable fuels
Alternative processing method: Ensilage of fish material processing plant
Alternative processing method: Multi-step catalytic processes for the production of renewable fuels plant
Alternative processing method: combustion of animal fat in a thermal boiler process plant
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 83 (a11y combobox e81; controls.txt name=establishment-type). First 20 options in DOM order (verified against the rendered <select>). Establishment type reference data — 97 types; fishery-relevant Factory Vessel / Fishing Vessel / Freezer Vessel / Freezing Vessel / Fresh Fishery Products Plant / Reefer Vessel exist in the full list but fall BEYOND the first 20 (they sort under 'F'/'R'). Source from reference-data service.

fields.name: order-by
fields.label: Sort by:
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 83 (a11y combobox e89; controls.txt name=order-by)

fields.name: add-id
fields.label: Select
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 83 — per-row submit <button name=add-id id=select-establishment-1 value={GUID}>; clicking navigates to .../establishment-of-origin. One button per result row carrying that establishment's GUID. Page object confirms the per-row Select and the `#select-establishment-1` id: SearchForApprovedEstablishmentPage.ts:22-28,46,68. Note (page object): 'Select' ADDS the chosen establishment to a running list and returns to the establishment-of-origin summary — it is not the page's terminal Continue (see save-and-continue field).

fields.name: save-and-continue
fields.label: Save and continue
fields.evidence: SearchForApprovedEstablishmentPage.ts:18-20 (btnSaveAndContinue = getByRole('button',{name:'Save and continue'})); driven at ched-p-workflows.ts:385,742-743,948-949 and ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:121 as the TERMINAL action once one or more establishments have been selected. NOT observed in trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d (that trace's captured action was the per-row Select navigation, not the final continue), so inferred not confirmed. This is the page's real 'continue' — the trace-derived spec had mislabelled the continue as 'Select'.

fields.name: search-for-an-approved-establishment
fields.label: Search for an approved establishment
fields.evidence: SearchForApprovedEstablishmentPage.ts:6-8 (btnSearchForApproved = getByRole('button',{name:'Search for an approved'})). Same button label as on the preceding Approved Establishment of Origin page (ApprovedEstablishmentOfOriginPage.ts:6-8). On the results page it re-opens/resets the search so a further establishment can be added — driven at ched-p-workflows.ts:376 inside the add-another loop, and ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:113. Supports the multi-select behaviour (see openQuestions). Not observed in the corpus trace.

openQuestions: Evidence-pointer/url-pattern mismatch: the brief's inferred url was /approved-establishment/search, but the observed page path is .../{chedReference}/veterinary-establishments (with ?establishment-country-code={cc}&page={n}), and the Select submit navigates to .../establishment-of-origin. The new IUU app must confirm the true route names.

No hint text and no HTML `required` attribute were rendered on any control — 'Country (required)' is a label convention only. Server-side validation copy for the required Country filter was not exercised in this trace, so 'required' is inferred from the label, not observed. Validation messages: gap.

The Country select defaults to the notification's country of origin, annotated '(country of origin)'. Country / Section / Type option lists are large establishment reference data (251 / 57 / 97 options) — the new app should source these from a reference-data service, not hardcode them. Only the first ~20 of each are transcribed.

IPAFFS renders results as a desktop govuk-table AND a duplicate mobile card layout (hence 20 Select buttons for 10 rows — two forms of select-establishment-1..10), plus bespoke pagination — none of these are Design System components. Whether the IUU rebuild needs a full approved-establishment search at all (vs a simpler lookup) is a scope question, given IUU is fishery-products-specific.

Continue-action discrepancy (finding): the trace-derived spec labelled the page's continue as 'Select', but the page object (SearchForApprovedEstablishmentPage.ts:18-20) and every workflow (ched-p-workflows.ts:385,742-743,948-949) show the terminal action is a separate 'Save and continue' button. 'Select' only ADDS a row to a running list of chosen establishments; 'Save and continue' finalises. The corpus trace happened to capture only the per-row Select navigation, not the final continue. Confirmed via test code, inferred (not trace-observed) for this page.

Multi-select behaviour the corpus never exercised: the page supports selecting MORE THAN ONE establishment before continuing. The workflow loops approvedEstablishmentCount times (ched-p-workflows.ts:369-385, config field lines 58-60, DEFAULT_CONFIG=1 at line 147-148), each iteration clicking 'Search for an approved establishment' → re-selecting Country → 'Search' → 'Select' another row, before 'Save and continue'. selectFirstEstablishmentNotStartingWithQuote (page object lines 50-82) skips already-chosen names and pages through results. The IUU rebuild must decide whether multiple approved establishments per notification are in scope.

Validation messages: gap. No trace rendered a validation/error message for this page, and no ched-p test asserts an error string on it (the page objects and workflows only drive happy-path selection). The behaviour of the required Country filter when Search is pressed empty, and of Save and continue with no establishment selected, is unobserved — a question for a human, not to be back-filled from legacy source.

Page-object corroboration summary: SearchForApprovedEstablishmentPage.ts exposes exactly these controls — dropdownCountry (line 10, 'Country (required)'), btnSearch (14), btnSaveAndContinue (18), the per-row Select via #select-establishment-1 / getByRole('button',{name:'Select'}) (22,27,46,68), btnSearchForApproved (6), and Pagination Next-page link (30-32). It exposes NO locator for the Name / Approval number / Section / Type / Status filters or the Sort controls — the tests never drive those, so their 'required:false' rests on trace evidence alone (the a11y label carried no HTML required attribute); this is consistent, not contradictory.
